domains.Business: What the platform does and the value it delivers: multi-market equity screening, backtesting, and research — signal-to-decision.
domains.Data: How data is modelled, stored, and governed: query-driven Cassandra (operational) + DuckDB warehouse (analytical) + point-in-time EDGAR + parquet seed.
domains.Application: The modules that realise capabilities: scanners, DVM/Trendlyne scoring, backtest engine, ML discovery, warehouse, apiclient.
domains.Technology: The runtime stack: Python, Cassandra, Kafka, Flink, DuckDB, yfinance/EDGAR/GLEIF, Git+LFS on macOS.

adm.platform: Signal-to-decision value stream; capabilities in USER_GUIDE
ARCHITECTURE_MAP.md component map; modular Python apps
togaf.py compliance checks + branch protection + signed commits
Signed git history, integrity manifest, CHANGELOG via commits

principles.name: Primacy of Principles
principles.statement: Every architectural decision on the platform is justified against these principles.
principles.rationale: Consistency and traceability of design choices across a large, evolving codebase.
principles.implications: New modules cite the principle(s) they uphold; togaf.py governs compliance.

principles.name: Research Integrity
principles.statement: Results are correct-by-construction and honestly caveated: no lookahead, costs and limitations stated.
principles.rationale: A research platform is only useful if its outputs are trustworthy.
principles.implications: Backtests are point-in-time; figures marked pre-cost; caveats documented per module.

principles.name: Data is a Shared, Query-Driven Asset
principles.statement: One analytical surface (DuckDB warehouse) over all data; tables modelled around read patterns.
principles.rationale: Avoids silos and CPU-heavy joins; one place to filter/aggregate/depict.
principles.implications: New results land as warehouse views/tables; Cassandra tables are partition/clustering-keyed.

principles.name: Cache-First Data Access
principles.statement: Market data is fetched at most once, then read locally; daily updates are incremental.
principles.rationale: Network fetches are the dominant cost and the main rate-limit risk.
principles.implications: All data scripts route OHLC through market_store.cached_download.

principles.name: Point-in-Time Data
principles.statement: Historical analysis uses only information known at the time (filed-date filtering).
principles.rationale: Eliminates lookahead bias, the classic backtest error.
principles.implications: pit_fundamentals filters EDGAR by filing date; fundamentals snapshots flagged as current-only.

principles.name: Data Quality at the Boundary
principles.statement: Untrusted external values are validated/cleaned on ingest (non-finite, dedup, clip outliers).
principles.rationale: Bad data (Infinity P/E, zero prices, penny spikes) silently corrupts results.
principles.implications: Non-finite guards in fundamentals; ±clip in viability; dedup before fetch.

principles.name: Reuse Over Rebuild
principles.statement: Shared concerns live in one module reused everywhere (data access, rate limiting, calendars).
principles.rationale: Single source of truth; fixes propagate; less duplicated risk.
principles.implications: market_store, apiclient, market_holidays, ml_signal_engine are shared building blocks.

principles.name: Loose Coupling — Operational vs Analytical
principles.statement: Separate the write/stream/point-lookup tier (Cassandra) from the bulk-analytics tier (DuckDB).
principles.rationale: Each store is used where it is strongest; scaling one doesn't disturb the other.
principles.implications: Cassandra for cache/CDC; DuckDB/parquet for scans and filtering.

principles.name: Controlled Interoperability (Rate Governance)
principles.statement: Every external call goes through one governed client with per-source throttling and backoff.
principles.rationale: Prevents rate-limit bans and keeps the system a good API citizen.
principles.implications: Fetchers call apiclient (yf_download/yf_info/http_get); no ad-hoc requests/yfinance.

principles.name: Security & Reproducibility
principles.statement: The repo is tamper-evident and reproducible: signed commits, integrity manifest, secrets via env only.
principles.rationale: Trust in the artifact and the ability to rebuild from source.
principles.implications: commit.gpgsign on; CHECKSUMS.sha256 + verify_integrity.sh; branch protection; no secrets in tree.
